title: Monash team identifies NOX4 in skeletal muscle as a master driver of biological aging — Science Advances
description: Monash Biomedicine Discovery Institute researchers published in Science Advances showing the muscle-resident protein NOX4 governs the metabolic and anti-aging response to exercise, with restored NOX4 levels reversing frailty-related muscle wasting in preclinical models. The team is now screening cruciferous-vegetable-derived compounds (broccoli, cabbage, brussels sprouts) to mimic the pathway pharmacologically — a potential exercise-mimetic geroprotector class.

title: FDA approves bemotrizinol (BEMT) — first new active sunscreen ingredient in 25+ years
description: The FDA approved bemotrizinol on June 10, the first new active sunscreen UV filter cleared in the US in over 25 years, offering broad UVA/UVB protection used in Europe and Asia for years. Photoaging is a leading driver of visible biological aging — the move materially upgrades the US topical longevity stack.

title: Manulife launches global Longevity Institute with $350M commitment
description: Manulife confirmed a $350M signature commitment behind its newly formed Longevity Institute, a global research, thought leadership and community-investment platform alongside its Asia Care Survey 2026 finding 13-14 years of late-life dependency is the average Asia-Pacific resident's biggest concern. The capital injection materially expands the institutional longevity-economy footprint outside biotech.

title: Continuous low-dose rapamycin suppresses age-related IL-17 γδ T-cell neuroinflammation
description: A PLOS ONE study covered by Fight Aging shows long-term low-dose dietary rapamycin selectively reduces IL-17-producing γδ T cells and blunts microglial inflammatory phenotype after endotoxin challenge in aged mice — without broad immunosuppression. The data give the daily low-dose camp new mechanistic support against the weekly pulse-dosing protocol.

title: Life Biosciences first patient dosed: media wave continues — Nature daily briefing, Morning Brew, Gizmodo
description: Coverage of Tuesday's first-ever administration of a cellular age-reversal therapy continued across mainstream outlets on Jun 10, with Nature's Daily Briefing, Morning Brew and Gizmodo amplifying ER-100's eight-week doxycycline-controlled reprogramming window. Field consensus is forming that the eye-first approach is the rational safety-bridge to broader systemic reprogramming applications.

title: Scientists target NOX4 protein in skeletal muscle to combat aging
description: Monash University team publishes in Science Advances showing NOX4 in skeletal muscle drives anti-aging exercise response; restoring NOX4 reverses muscle wasting in preclinical models and team now screening cruciferous-vegetable compounds as exercise mimetics.

title: Manulife Asia Care Survey 2026: Independence as Asia's new legacy
description: Survey of 9,000+ adults across 9 Asian markets shows respondents estimate 13-14 years of later life may require care/financial support; Manulife backs $350M Longevity Institute focused on extending healthy years and financial resilience.

title: FDA approves bemotrizinol — first new sunscreen active ingredient in 25+ years
description: FDA's first new active sunscreen ingredient since 2000 — BEMT offers broad UVA/UVB protection, used in Europe/Asia for years; significant upgrade to US photoaging-prevention stack.

title: Ingenix raises €13M from Sofinnova Partners-led syndicate
description: Sofinnova Partners leads €13M round for Ingenix to scale modality-fusion novel architecture — illustrative of continued private capital flow into AI-enabled biotech adjacent to longevity drug discovery.

title: Daily briefing: Trial to 'de-age' cells treats first person
description: Nature Daily Briefing names Life Biosciences' first ER-100 dose as a defining moment for partial-reprogramming therapeutics.

title: Life Biosciences (ER-100) Phase 1 trial: first patient dosed
description: NCT07290244 Phase 1 trial of OCT4/SOX2/KLF4 reprogramming gene therapy in glaucoma/NAION; under 20 patients across Boston, NYC, LA, Charleston; doxycycline on/off control with 8-week activation window.

title: World-first: therapy to make cells young again trialled in a person
description: Nature feature; Kaeberlein notes the eye is a wise first organ choice given lower catastrophic side-effect risk than other tissues.

title: First Human Receives Experimental Therapy to Reverse Cellular Aging
description: Gizmodo details ER-100's three-factor approach skipping the cancer-associated fourth Yamanaka factor, and the doxycycline-controlled activation switch.

title: Long-term rapamycin treatment suppresses IL-17 γδ T cells and blunts neuroinflammation
description: Discussion of new PLOS ONE paper showing continuous low-dose dietary rapamycin in aged mice selectively reduces IL-17-producing γδ T cells in peritoneal cavity and attenuates microglia inflammatory response.

title: 40Hz audio triggers massive amyloid clearance in aging monkeys
description: Forum-aggregated coverage of a new primate study showing 40Hz gamma-frequency auditory stimulation produces meaningful amyloid clearance in aging non-human primates — translational signal for non-drug Alzheimer's protocols.

title: Oral sodium hyaluronate improves skin hydration, barrier function and signs of aging — 150-person RCT
description: Randomized double-blind placebo-controlled trial in 150 healthy adults reports oral sodium hyaluronate improved skin hydration, barrier function and signs of aging — adds to consumer-supplement evidence base.

title: Glucosamine accelerates dementia — new analysis
description: Forum discussion of an analysis suggesting glucosamine supplementation accelerates dementia risk, contrary to earlier protective signals — important nuance for the joint-supplement-using longevity audience.

title: 60 Minutes: Dog Aging Project rapamycin trial could unlock secrets for people
description: NIH-funded large-scale rapamycin RCT in pet dogs; hundreds dosed in active arm with healthspan endpoints; mainstream coverage builds public/regulator momentum.

title: PEARL trial: rapamycin slows muscle and bone aging in first long-term human RCT
description: 115 adults 50-85 on 10 mg/week rapamycin for 48 weeks; muscle preservation in women, bone preservation in men, no serious adverse events.

title: Stanfield-Kaeberlein rapamycin RCT: 6 mg/week sirolimus on strength and endurance
description: BMC-registered double-blind RCT in 40 adults aged 65-80 over 13 weeks; strength and endurance primary endpoints; next translational checkpoint for low-dose rapamycin.

title: UT San Antonio launches multi-phase rapamycin study in older adults
description: Dean Kellogg Jr. (UT Health San Antonio Geriatrics) leads multi-phase clinical study to understand rapamycin's cardiovascular and healthspan effects in older adults.

title: Albert Einstein College of Medicine launches BIO-VITAL gerotherapeutics validation program
description: BIO-VITAL connects biotech/pharma partners with Einstein's three integrated cores (cellular hallmarks, preclinical models, human longevity multi-omics) backed by Ofer Family Foundation.

title: Rejuvenate Bio announces $6M financing and strategic R&D collaboration with Merck Animal Health
description: $6M round led by VCapital with Merck Animal Health, Kendall Capital Partners, Connecticut Innovations and Digitalis backing continued gene-therapy platform development.

title: Top Longevity Startups by Fundraising (2026)
description: Industry rundown of the highest-funded private longevity startups in 2026, providing reference point for relative valuation and capital concentration.

title: Contrary Research: The Anti-Aging Therapeutics Boom
description: Investor-facing market memo framing partial reprogramming, senolytics, GLP-1 aging trials, AI drug discovery and consumer wearables as a structural investable boom.

title: Nature 2026 master review: therapeutic strategies for aging and age-associated diseases
description: Comprehensive review integrating senolytics, senomorphics, senoreversion, metabolic/dietary interventions, biomarkers, multiomics and AI into one unified geroscience framework.

title: Sinclair + Tally Health: first comprehensive evidence map of human epigenetic aging interventions
description: Frontiers in Genetics review identifies exercise, semaglutide, pitavastatin, plant-rich diet, caloric restriction, omega-3, multivitamin as interventions associated with favorable next-gen epigenetic clock changes (41 human studies).

title: UCSD: Semaglutide may slow biological aging in randomized HIV lipohypertrophy trial
description: First RCT showing semaglutide slowed DunedinPACE by 9% and PCGrimAge mortality clock relative to placebo over 32 weeks; published in Nature Communications.

title: NewLimit raises $435M Series C at $3.1B valuation
description: Founders Fund-led round brings the Brian Armstrong-founded cell-reprogramming company to $3.1B ahead of its first liver age-reversal human trial.

title: Why Longevity Has Become The World's New Obsession
description: Video essay on the mainstream cultural pivot toward longevity science, products, and protocols — high-engagement industry framing piece.
